summary: Stage-I laryngeal cancer can be treated effectively by either surgery or radiotherapy; however, current literature suggests that careful selection may allow certain surgeries such as TLM or TORS to provide similar curative results but also maintain vocal functions more successfully than standard radiotherapy treatments. Given these factors, prioritizing an approach likely to retain speech capabilities appears reasonable when considering all aspects including efficacy against recurrence. Based upon available scientific consensus indicating no significant difference between methods except possibly improved functional outcome post-surgically via modern approaches, recommending \(\textbf{surgical intervention}\) remains justified under most circumstances given patient suitability criteria are met properly during evaluation phases leading up to definitive management decisions
